abstract.para: System and application tracing provides deep insight into how binaries interact with the &sles; kernel and user-space libraries. By monitoring execution patterns, system administrators can isolate configuration defects, permission errors, and performance regressions.

topic.para: Tracing utilities introduce execution overhead by intercepting execution states. Avoid running continuous traces on high-throughput production environments unless executing short, focused diagnostic captures.

section.title: Tracing system calls with strace
section.para: The strace utility intercepts and records system calls made by a process and the signals it receives. It is ideal for identifying missing files, blocked I/O operations, or network communication failures.

step.para: Locate the Process ID (PID) of the service you want to monitor.
Attach strace to the running process using the -p flag:
Alternatively, capture summary statistics by running the process directly from launch with the -c flag:
Stop the capture at any time by pressing CtrlC. Review the generated table for a breakdown of errors, call counts, and execution time percentages.

section.title: Monitoring library intersections with itrace
section.para: While strace captures kernel-level interactions, ltrace intercepts and logs dynamic library calls called by user-space programs. Use this to verify that a binary is invoking the correct functions within shared libraries (.so files).

step.para: Run ltrace directly against an executable file.
To narrow down the output, use the -e flag followed by the function or library pattern to isolate specific symbols:
Redirect complex output strings to an external log file for historical post-mortem analysis:

section.title: Detecting Memory Leakages with Valgrind
section.para: Valgrind acts as an abstraction framework providing an array of profiling engines. The default engine, memcheck, serves as an instrumentation tool to uncover memory corruption, buffer overflows, and unreleased pointers.

step.para: Compile your debugging binary with debug symbols enabled (-g) whenever possible for precise line-number mapping.
Launch the application inside the Valgrind virtualization layer using the memory check flag:
Inspect the printed termination manifest. Review the LEAK SUMMARY section to verify the exact bytes categorization categorized as definitely lost, indirectly lost, or still reachable.

listitem.para: Problem:When attempting to attach to a process via strace -p or ltrace -p, you receive an error: attach: ptrace(PTRACE_ATTACH, ...): Operation not permitted.
Solution: &productnameshort; tightens default security properties via the ptrace_scope kernel control. Temporarily permit standard process attachments by adjusting the runtime control:
To persist this modification across reboots, update /etc/sysctl.d/99-tuning.conf.
